title: Zhong Lin (ZL) Wang interview: Energy generation using ...
text:
hello my name is joh l ZL1 from Georgia
Tech I'm a professor at Georgia Tech uh
my research involve nanom materials
nanot technology with applications in
nanom medical nano energy and
electronics our research involves
several parts the first part is energ
energ conversion we are develop
technology to convert mechanical energy
for example your body movement energy
noises
vibrations and any kind of tiny physical
movement into electricity for what we
try to use this energy to power tiny
little electron devices the goal of
nanot technology to make device smaller
better and smarter but once the size
become small the energy requirements
also become small in a lot of case when
the volume becomes small battery is not
the best choice and we are thinking can
we use the energy available in our
environment to power such devices so
that for the environmental application
such as in BIO sensors inv Viv bio
sensors uh environmental monitoring and
even some kind of personal health care
like building structure monitoring etc
those environment requires the energy
conversion from mechanical energy into
electricity for power some devices our
future is complete wireless self-powered
Nano devices this is the first research
the second research we involve build
electronic devices we call
nanotronics this is about utilize force
or pressure triggered electronic process
to fabricate new transistors new diode
that's very sensitive to the force you
appli it such a device can be utilized
in future for strain sensors strain
switches electronic signatures and many
more the third research we involved in
is biomedical we try to characterization
the cell mechanical properties
electrical properties can we use the
electrical or mechanical characteristic
of normal cells cancer cells to form a
single cell diagnostic technology the
fourth research we involve in is bi
inspired process this have two parts one
is Bier photonics or smart biop
photonics for example like a butterfly
wings can we replicate butterfly wings
and tune the dialectric function
function so we can make smarter photonic
structures the second part of this
project also involves the bio
inspired biological process for example
we're mimicking the uh foot of the GLE
this tiny little animal and this the
largest animal can hand s upside down on
the ceiling can we make the food used
nanom materials and the goal is that to
for interconnects space technology in
the in the future so we so far we have
used carbon nanot tube to make this kind
of structures we can hand 100 Newton per
cm square that means that for a size 5x
5 cm of such structure we can hand a man
of 100 kilogram on the wall this the
fourth research the the fifth research
we involve functional materials this
means the materials that have magnetic
Ferro electric p Electric
multifunctionality our goals to
integrate these materials into devices
so that our future is
involved smaller better multifunctional
systems that's the goal of our research
thank you
